copernicus_soil_water.label: Copernicus satellite soil-water proxy
limitations: Regional proxy, satellite/model product, not an in-situ peat water-table sensor.
Copernicus SWI is emitted as an independent proxy and is not used to derive peat water-table depth.
Hydrological pressure classes are not derived from SWI at this stage.
Raw valid values are 0..200 and decoded with scale_factor 0.5 to percent; fill value 255 and flags 241, 242, 251, 252, 253 and 254 are unavailable, never converted to zero.
copernicus_soil_water.product: Soil Water Index 2025-present (raster 1 km), Europe, daily – version 2
copernicus_soil_water.selection_method: Deterministically select the nearest/containing EPSG:4326 1/112° Copernicus SWI grid cell to Wiesmoor (53.4167, 7.7333); no averaging, no conversion to peat water-table depth.

source.access_method: Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem via Copernicus Browser, OData API, or S3; this adapter does not guess collection identifiers or file paths without verified anonymous discovery.
source.dataset: Soil Water Index 2025-present (raster 1 km), Europe, daily – version 2
source.name: Copernicus Land Monitoring Service

adapter_errors: Live Copernicus SWI discovery/download is not enabled: anonymous CDSE product discovery was not verified in this environment, and the adapter must not guess OData collection identifiers or file paths.

groundwater_proxy.interpretation_note: Nearby groundwater stations can indicate regional groundwater behaviour but are not an in-situ peat water-table sensor for Wiesmoor-Nord.
groundwater_proxy.label: groundwater proxy
groundwater_proxy.selection_method: Fetch official NLWKN Grundwasserstandonline public station metadata, parse station coordinates and current values when present, then select the nearest suitable stations to Wiesmoor (53.4167, 7.7333) by haversine distance; prefer stations with live value/date/status metadata but never fabricate missing values.

source.dataset: Grundwasserstandonline public station metadata with current daily value where exposed
source.name: NLWKN Grundwasserstandonline / groundwater monitoring data

peat_context.drainage_context: MoorIS notes that Nordgeorgsfehnkanal and Großefehnkanal cross the moor and serve moor drainage; drainage and road networks in parts of the moor are described as moderately developed.
peat_context.extraction_history: MoorIS reports peat extraction for the Wiesmoor power plant from 1921, a documented 1952 maximum annual fuel-peat extraction figure for the power-plant operation, industrial black-peat extraction in northern Klingemoor, and widespread partial extraction at margins; extraction areas for the power plant are noted as lying in the Wiesmoor-Süd description area.
peat_context.land_use_history: MoorIS describes Wiesmoor as shaped by peat-fired power generation, horticulture/greenhouses, settlement roads along canals, agriculture dominated by grassland, smaller arable/garden areas, and later trade, light industry and tourism.
limitations: MoorIS/NLWKN context is static descriptive source material, not a live sensor.
No numeric peat thickness, peatland area, water-table depth or hydrological pressure class is derived from this context layer.

Some source statements refer to subareas such as Klingemoor, Wiesmoor-Süd or the nearby Wiesmoor-Klinge protected area; these are kept as contextual limitations rather than generalized to all Wiesmoor-Nord soils.
German source terminology is summarized in English; source-native meanings are preserved conservatively.
peat_context.moor_type_context: MoorIS/NLWKN place Wiesmoor-Nord in the East Frisian central raised-bog (Hochmoor) landscape; nearby NLWKN Wiesmoor-Klinge is described as part of the Hochmoorkomplex der ostfriesischen Zentralmoore.

peat_thickness_context.description: No numeric peat-thickness value is emitted because this observer does not yet have a reproducible official machine-readable extraction for the Wiesmoor-Nord peat-thickness layer.

peat_context.reproducibility_note: The observer embeds concise summaries from official MoorIS/NLWKN pages and preserves URLs/identifiers. Re-running the observer does not fetch MoorIS, so live adapter status and API attempts are unaffected.
peat_context.restoration_or_management_context: NLWKN describes the nearby state-owned Wiesmoor-Klinge protected area northwest of Wiesmoor as formerly extensively cut over, with rewetting initiated before designation and high-bog habitats redeveloping in central areas; this is supporting local context, not a numeric Wiesmoor-Nord measurement.

source.name: MoorIS Niedersachsen / NLWKN Moorschutzprogramm entry 377 Wiesmoor-Nord
source.note: Static embedded context; no live request is needed during observer runs.
source.publisher: Niedersächsischer Landesbetrieb für Wasserwirtschaft, Küsten- und Naturschutz (NLWKN) / Landesamt für Bergbau, Energie und Geologie (LBEG)

peat_context.source_name: MoorIS Niedersachsen / NLWKN Moorschutzprogramm entry 377 Wiesmoor-Nord
peat_context.why_this_area_matters: Wiesmoor-Nord is an official Moorschutzprogramm/MoorIS area entry (377) in the East Frisian central moor landscape, where drainage, peat extraction, settlement, agriculture and restoration context all affect interpretation of regional hydrological proxies.

extracted_fields: supporting local restoration context from NLWKN Wiesmoor-Klinge
wiesmoor_nord.notes: Numeric values in the source text that describe historical operations or a neighboring protected area are kept textual and are not emitted as peat-thickness or area fields.
wiesmoor_nord.page_or_dataset_identifier: MoorIS page pgId=585; Moorschutzprogramm area 377 Wiesmoor-Nord

limitations: Regional proxy observation — not an in-situ peat water-table sensor.
No peat-water-table depth is measured or inferred.
NLWKN groundwater stations are regional groundwater proxies; station distance and local hydrogeological representativeness are uncertain.
DWD gridded soil moisture under grass is a regional model product for the 0-60 cm layer and is not converted to peat water-table depth.
DWD weather pressure comes from the selected nearby daily climate station; station distance and weather gradients limit site representativeness.
Copernicus SWI metadata-only and MoorIS static context are not used as live pressure signals.
peatland_hydrological_pressure.methodology: Conservative observer heuristic using only currently live proxy groups: DWD weather pressure, DWD gridded soil moisture under grass 0-60 cm, and NLWKN regional groundwater status. At least two independent valid live proxy groups are required. Elevated/high is emitted only when at least two live groups align toward dry/low-water or wet/high-water pressure; mixed or weak signals default to normal. Copernicus SWI metadata-only and MoorIS static context are excluded from the live classification.
peatland_hydrological_pressure.source_interpretation_note: Interpret value as a conservative multi-source regional proxy pressure state for Wiesmoor, not as measured site hydrology.

thresholds.groundwater_proxy: Uses NLWKN source status_category/regional_status_summary as low, very_low, normal, high, or very_high without reclassifying the measured metre value.

regional_soil_water.note: Provisional Wiesmoor Observer heuristic bands in source-native DWD AMBAV ‰ nFK; not official peatland classes and not peat water-table depth.

weather_pressure.dry: 7-day rainfall < 5 mm and 30-day rainfall < 35 mm plus dry-day or heat support
weather_pressure.very_dry: 7-day rainfall < 2 mm and 30-day rainfall < 20 mm plus dry-day or heat support

regional_soil_water.dataset: Daily grids of mean soil moisture under grass for Germany, Version v1.0
regional_soil_water.label: regional DWD gridded soil-moisture condition
limitations: Regional gridded model product, not an in-situ peat water-table sensor.
DWD AMBAV soil moisture under grass preserves source-native ‰ nFK semantics and is not converted to water-table depth.
regional_soil_water.selection_method: Deterministically select the source grid cell containing or nearest to Wiesmoor (53.4167, 7.7333) from the DWD NetCDF grid metadata; no regional averaging and no conversion to peat water-table depth.

source.dataset: Daily grids of mean soil moisture under grass for Germany, Version v1.0
source.name: Deutscher Wetterdienst (DWD) Climate Data Center

regional_soil_water.variable: mean soil moisture under grass, 0-60 cm layer

sources.name: MoorIS Niedersachsen / NLWKN Moorschutzprogramm entry 377 Wiesmoor-Nord
sources.note: Static embedded context; no live request is needed during observer runs.
sources.publisher: Niedersächsischer Landesbetrieb für Wasserwirtschaft, Küsten- und Naturschutz (NLWKN) / Landesamt für Bergbau, Energie und Geologie (LBEG)

sources.name: NLWKN Grundwasserstandonline / groundwater monitoring data

sources.access_method: Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem via Copernicus Browser, OData API, or S3; this adapter does not guess collection identifiers or file paths without verified anonymous discovery.
sources.dataset: Soil Water Index 2025-present (raster 1 km), Europe, daily – version 2
sources.name: Copernicus Land Monitoring Service

sources.dataset: Recent daily climate observations Germany (KL), daily precipitation RSK and daily mean temperature TMK
sources.name: Deutscher Wetterdienst (DWD) Climate Data Center

summary: Wiesmoor peatland hydrological pressure remains unavailable as an in-situ conclusion; DWD daily rainfall is emitted when available as a regional weather proxy component only.

coverage.dry_day_rule: A dry day is a valid DWD daily precipitation (RSK) observation below 1.0 mm/day; exactly 1.0 mm/day is not dry. Missing markers and absent dates are unavailable, never dry.
coverage.minimum_coverage_rule: 7-day total requires 7/7 valid precipitation days; 30-day total requires at least 27/30 valid precipitation days. DWD -999/missing and absent dates are unavailable, not zero.

weather_pressure.label: weather pressure

source.dataset: Recent daily climate observations Germany (KL), daily precipitation RSK and daily mean temperature TMK
source.name: Deutscher Wetterdienst (DWD) Climate Data Center

station.selection_method: Nearest DWD CDC recent daily climate station to Wiesmoor (53.4167, 7.7333) with station metadata end date within 14 days; distance uses haversine great-circle calculation.

weather_pressure.temperature_label: latest valid daily mean temperature (TMK), not an instantaneous/current temperature
